entity.label: Joktan
entity.subtitle: Male
entity.description: Little, the second of the two sons of Eber (Gen. 10:25; 1 Chr. 1:19). There is an Arab tradition that Joktan (Arab. Kahtan) was the progenitor of all the purest tribes of Central and Southern Arabia.

nodes.label: Joktan
nodes.description: Little, the second of the two sons of Eber (Gen. 10:25; 1 Chr. 1:19). There is an Arab tradition that Joktan (Arab. Kahtan) was the progenitor of all the purest tribes of Central and Southern Arabia.

nodes.label: Eber (Heber)
nodes.description: The third post-duluvian patriach after Shem (Gen. 10:24; 11:14). He is regarded as the founder of the Hebrew race (10:21; Num. 24:24). In Luke 3:35 he is called Heber.

nodes.label: Almodad
nodes.description: Immeasurable, the first named of the sons of Joktan (Gen. 10:26), the founder of an Arabian tribe.

nodes.label: Sheba (son of Joktan)
nodes.description: Heb. id. A son of Joktan (Gen. 10:28), probably the founder of the Sabeans.

nodes.label: Jobab (son of Joktan)
nodes.description: One of the sons of Joktan, and founder of an Arabian tribe (Gen. 10:29).

nodes.label: Ebal (son of Joktan)
nodes.description: A descendant of Eber (1 Chr. 1:22), called also Obal (Gen. 10:28).

nodes.label: Ophir
nodes.description: One of the sons of Joktan (Gen. 10:29).

nodes.label: Uzal
nodes.description: A wanderer, a descendant of Joktan (Gen. 10:27; 1 Chr. 1:21), the founder apparently of one of the Arab tribes; the name also probably of the province they occupied and of their chief city.

nodes.label: Hadoram
nodes.description: The fifth son of Joktan, the founder of an Arab tribe (Gen. 10:27; 1 Chr. 1:21).

nodes.label: Abimael
nodes.description: Father of Mael, one of the sons or descendants of Joktan, in Northern Arabia (Gen. 10:28; 1 Chr. 1:22).

nodes.label: Hazarmaveth
nodes.description: Court of death, the third son of Joktan, and a region in Arabia-Felix settled by him (Gen. 10:26; 1 Chr. 1:20). It is probably the modern province of Hadramaut, situated on the Indian Ocean east of the modern Yemen.

nodes.label: Peleg
nodes.description: Division, one of the sons of Eber; so called because “in his days was the earth divided” (Gen. 10:25). Possibly he may have lived at the time of the dispersion from Babel. But more probably the reference is to the dispersion of the two races which sprang from Eber, the one spreading towards Mesopotamia and Syria, and the other southward into Arabia.

verses.text: And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg; for in his days was the earth divided; and his brother’s name was Joktan.
And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,
And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these were the sons of Joktan.
And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg; because in his days the earth was divided: and his brother’s name was Joktan.
And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,
And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab. All these were the sons of Joktan.
